cards.back: A program in execution, with its own memory space and resources
The discipline of selecting the next process to be executed and allocating system resources for its execution
A scheduling algorithm that allocates a fixed time quantum to each process, switching to the next process after the quantum expires
The process of switching from one process to another, involving the saving of the current state and loading of the new state
The time taken to switch from one process to another, depending on the complexity of the process and the system's hardware capabilities
